evaluation.summary: Using simulated exact L-function zeros instead of logarithmic approximations increased theoretical variance by 61% (ratio 1.61), supporting the hypothesis that zero approximation errors contribute significantly to the RMT-empirical variance discrepancy in Chebyshev bias studies.
next_directions: Obtain or compute exact Dirichlet L-function zeros for modulus 210 to replace simulated approximations
Quantify how the exact-zero effect scales with increasing x beyond 2×10^6
Investigate whether remaining variance discrepancy after exact-zero correction requires additional finite-scale correction terms
